title: This Asset Class Has Lagged the Market for Years But Was the Best Performer in June. Time to Invest?
author: Matthew Benjamin
description: Real estate investment trusts (REITs) rebounded strongly in June and are up 9.5% in 2026, outperforming the broader market. After years of underperformance due to post-pandemic trends and elevated interest rates, REITs are benefiting from workers returning to offices and malls, data center growth driven by AI, and moderating interest rates. Key performing categories include lodging REITs (up 43%), data center REITs (up 33%), and healthcare REITs (up 20%), while only gaming and telecommunications REITs are down for the year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Up 9.5% in 2026, outperforming S&P 500; highly diversified across 145 REITs with strong exposure to growth sectors like data centers (10.7%) and healthcare (16.5%)

title: Want a Lifetime of Passive Income? Buy Realty Income Stock in July and Never Sell.
author: James Brumley
description: Realty Income (O), a REIT specializing in brick-and-mortar retail properties, is presented as a long-term dividend investment opportunity. Despite retail sector challenges, the company maintains 98%+ occupancy rates with strong tenants like Walmart and Home Depot, has paid monthly dividends since 1969 with quarterly increases since 1998, and is exploring new markets including AI data center infrastructure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates resilience with 98%+ occupancy rates, consistent monthly dividend payments since 1969, quarterly dividend increases since 1998, 13.6% compound annual returns since 1994 IPO, and strategic diversification into AI data center partnerships.
Mentioned as a top-20 tenant of Realty Income with both brick-and-mortar and e-commerce operations, demonstrating adaptability to changing retail landscape.

title: Rising AI and HPC Workloads Propel Demand for Liquid Cooling in European Data Centers
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Europe's data center construction market is expected to grow from $16.99 billion in 2025 to $58.74 billion by 2031, driven by liquid cooling adoption for AI workloads, digitalization, cloud expansion, and government support. Hyperscale data centers lead investments, with Western Europe and the Nordics dominating due to renewable energy availability and strategic locations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a key infrastructure provider in the expanding European data center market, positioned to benefit from investments in electrical and mechanical infrastructure.
Highlighted as a major investor and market player expanding infrastructure in Europe, including new cable stations in Ireland, directly benefiting from market growth.

title: Digital Realty Prices Secondary Offering of Common Stock by Blackstone
author: Na
description: Digital Realty announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 12.3 million shares by Blackstone affiliates at $185 per share. The offering is contingent on Digital Realty's acquisition of Blackstone's interests in two Northern Virginia data center joint ventures (Digital Carver Dulles 9 and Digital Carver Brickyard), expected to close on June 30, 2026. Digital Realty will not receive proceeds from the secondary offering.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is expanding its data center portfolio by acquiring Blackstone's interests in two Northern Virginia facilities, strengthening its position as the largest global data center provider. The acquisition demonstrates growth and strategic expansion in a key market.

title: Digital Realty Launches ServiceFabric® MCP, Bringing AI-Native Programmable Control to 800+ Data Centers
author: Digital Realty
description: Digital Realty announced the availability of ServiceFabric® Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open protocol that makes infrastructure programmable for Private AI environments across its 800+ global data centers. The platform provides AI-native control surfaces for design, provisioning, discovery, security, and operations integration, enabling enterprises to deploy and manage AI infrastructure at scale without vendor lock-in.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company announced a significant new product launch (ServiceFabric MCP) that extends its platform capabilities for enterprise AI deployments across 800+ data centers. The announcement demonstrates technological innovation, strategic positioning in the growing AI infrastructure market, and validation from customers and partners. The open-by-design approach and broad ecosystem partnerships strengthen competitive positioning.
